title: Hieracium macranthum
taxonomy: Hieracium macranthum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is a member of the genus Hieracium, which is commonly known as hawkweed. The genus Hieracium is divided into several subgenera and sections, with Hieracium macranthum being part of the subgenus Pilosella. This classification is based on the plant's morphology and phylogenetic studies. The species is also sometimes placed in the subgenus Hieracium, depending on the source and taxonomic treatment.
uses: Hieracium macranthum is a plant species that has been used in traditional medicine in various parts of the world. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help in treating wounds, skin conditions, and infections. The plant's leaves and flowers are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that are said to have medicinal benefits. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Hieracium macranthum for medicinal purposes. Some studies suggest that the plant may also have antioxidant properties, which could help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress.

side_effects: Hieracium macranthum, also known as yellow hawkweed, is a plant that is not typically consumed for medicinal purposes. intro: Hieracium macranthum, commonly known as large hawk's beard, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium macranthum include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions.
